questions.question: Explain the concept of encapsulation and de-encapsulation in the OSI model.
keyPoints: Encapsulation: adding headers/trailers at each layer as data moves down from Application to Physical
De-encapsulation: removing headers at each layer as data moves up from Physical to Application
PDUs: Data → Segment → Packet → Frame → Bits (and reverse)

questions.question: Differentiate between TCP and UDP protocols.
keyPoints: TCP: Connection-oriented, reliable, error recovery, flow control, slower
UDP: Connectionless, unreliable, no error recovery, faster
TCP used for web, email; UDP for video streaming, DNS

questions.question: Explain the functions of the Network Layer (Layer 3).
keyPoints: Logical addressing using IP addresses
Routing - determining best path for data
Packet forwarding
Functions of routers operating at this layer

questions.question: What are the responsibilities of the Presentation Layer?
keyPoints: Data translation/formatting between systems
Encryption and decryption for security
Data compression to reduce bandwidth
Format conversion: text, images, audio, video

questions.question: List the functions of the Data Link Layer.
keyPoints: Node-to-node (hop-by-hop) delivery
Physical addressing using MAC addresses
Framing data into transmittable units
Error detection (CRC) and flow control
Sub-layers: LLC and MAC

questions.question: Explain the difference between logical and physical addressing with examples.
keyPoints: Logical (IP): Network layer, software-assigned, used for routing across networks, can be changed
Physical (MAC): Data Link layer, hardcoded in NIC, used for local delivery, 48-bit address
Example: IP is like street address, MAC is like house number

questions.question: Write a note on devices operating at different OSI layers.
keyPoints: Layer 1: Hub, Repeater - transmit bits
Layer 2: Switch, Bridge - forward frames using MAC
Layer 3: Router - forward packets using IP
Layer 4+: Gateway - connects different networks/protocols

questions.question: Draw and explain the 7-layer OSI model with functions of each layer and example protocols.
keyPoints: Diagram showing 7 layers with numbering
Layer 7 Application: Network services to apps (HTTP, FTP, DNS, SMTP)
Layer 6 Presentation: Data translation, encryption (JPEG, SSL, TLS)
Layer 5 Session: Session management (RPC, NetBIOS)
Layer 4 Transport: End-to-end, reliability (TCP, UDP)
Layer 3 Network: Routing, logical addressing (IP, ICMP)
Layer 2 Data Link: Node-to-node, MAC (Ethernet, PPP)
Layer 1 Physical: Bit transmission (cables, hubs)
Examples at each layer

questions.question: Explain the data flow in the OSI model when a user requests a webpage from a server.
keyPoints: User types URL in browser (Application Layer)
DNS resolves domain name (Application)
HTTP sends request (Application)
Data formatted/encrypted (Presentation)
Session established (Session)
TCP segments data (Transport)
IP adds logical addresses, routes (Network)
Ethernet frames data with MAC (Data Link)
Converted to bits, transmitted (Physical)
Reverse process at server: bits→frames→segments→data

questions.question: Compare the OSI model with the TCP/IP model and explain why OSI is called a reference model.
keyPoints: TCP/IP has 4 layers: Application, Transport, Internet, Link; OSI has 7 layers
OSI provides clearer separation of functions
OSI is theoretical/reference, TCP/IP is practical/implementation
OSI was developed first as standard, TCP/IP emerged from ARPANET
OSI helps understand networking conceptually; TCP/IP is actual protocol suite used in internet
OSI not implemented fully; TCP/IP forms basis of internet

questions.question: Describe the role of each type of network device in relation to OSI layers.
keyPoints: Hub/Repeater: Layer 1 - amplify/regenerate signals, no addressing
Bridge/Switch: Layer 2 - forward frames using MAC address table, create LAN segments
Router: Layer 3 - forward packets using IP address, connect different networks
Gateway: Layer 4-7 - connect different protocol stacks, translate between networks
Each device operates at specific layer(s) and understands corresponding addressing
